_meta.purpose: The clamp: the human's signed disposition of every governed knob in the Versus Table. The gate holds the live RULES (stable/versus-match.js) against this record — any governed knob that changes without a matching edit here turns the gate red. The measurement (the balance tournament) may PROPOSE a value; shipping it requires transcribing it into this record. A diode: current flows only after the human closes the switch.
_meta.boundary: Procedural, not cryptographic. The gate enforces that RULES equals this record; it cannot verify WHO edited the file. The value is not identity — it is that no governed knob can move without a deliberate, visible edit to a disposition document, which forces a human moment where a silent auto-tune used to be.
_meta.how_to_change_a_knob: 1) run the tournament, read its proposed value; 2) edit RULES in versus-match.js; 3) edit 'governed' here to match; 4) if scoring changed, bump scale_version + re-freeze scale-fixture.json (updating scale_fixture_sha256); 5) APPEND a new signoff_log entry with ratified:true, a real ratification_token, scale = the current version, and governed_sha256 = the sha256 of the sorted-key-canonical 'governed' block. ENFORCED (2026-07-16): the gate goes red unless the NEWEST entry ratifies the EXACT current governed state — no knob moves without a fresh, hash-pinned human disposition. Still procedural, not cryptographic identity (see boundary); a forger can write a matching ratified entry, but nothing moves silently.

signoff_log.action: Clamp installed. Current governed values brought under the signed record.
signoff_log.note: The 0.2 sound-reward tuning — floor((probes-par)/2) — was set by the balance tournament, not by a recorded human disposition. It is UNRATIFIED. The clamp is now live regardless: from this point no governed knob changes without a new signoff_log entry. To ratify the 0.2 value, Jake sets ratified:true and a ratification_token (his initials). To revert instead, restore probes-par and re-baseline. Either is a conscious act; the auto-tune path is closed.

signoff_log.action: RATIFIED. Jake accepted the 0.2 value as his recorded disposition (directed via chat this session), before pushing the Stable.
signoff_log.note: Ratifies sound-reward = floor((probes-par)/2) as the accepted CURRENT value — converting the 2026-07-15 auto-tune into a signed human disposition, closing the governance gap the red team flagged. Scope is honest: this ratifies the value IN PLACE, not that it is optimal. The root-cause fix (raise `par` to reflect a rational hidden-information reader's true probe cost, per witness-suite/SPEC-B-holdout.md) remains OPEN future work; ratifying 0.2 does not close it. The token is a human-readable marker directed by Jake, not cryptographic identity (see _meta.boundary); Jake may amend it to any mark he prefers.

signoff_log.action: RATIFIED scale 0.3 — Versus Table migrated to the region+extremes target model (witness-suite v0.3.1), with one tournament-measured re-tune. Jake directed the migration via chat this session.
signoff_log.note: Scope, stated honestly: (1) PAR SEMANTICS changed — par is now the minimal cover (boundaries + distinct extremes + interiors of uncovered regions), derived through the suite, replacing special.length; 0.2 ledgers are refused by replay rather than silently re-rated. (2) ONE FORMULA CHANGED: the sound-builder reward divisor, floor((probes-par)/2) -> floor((probes-par)/3). Measured, not chosen by taste: under derived par the /2 reward let defensive sound-spam out-earn fair play again (193.6 vs 187.6 in the reference tournament — the exact imbalance the 0.2 tuning existed to prevent); /3 restores the reference players' 0.2-era builder economics and the balance invariants. FLAGGED FOR JAKE: this divisor is the one judgment call in the migration — revert or amend at will; the tournament re-measures either way. (3) The surge protector gained a STALL trip (no new target for 2x SURGE_GRACE_PROBES, derived from the existing grace constant — no new governed knob); required because region credit gives even unaimed play small R, blinding a Gamma-only trip. (4) Closes the SPEC-B-holdout par root-cause item by derivation. Token is a human-readable marker directed by Jake via chat (0.2-ratification precedent); he may amend it.

signoff_log.action: RATIFIED — Spec B built (witness-suite/SPEC-B-holdout.md). certify_roster_sha256 added to governed, pinning stable/roster-certify.js's file content (the generated hold-out grid + its declarations + the grammar that produces them). Jake directed the build via chat this session.
signoff_log.note: This ratifies that the CERTIFY ROSTER EXISTS, is frozen, and is disjoint (by id and by canonical declaration content) from the tune roster it was never fit to — it does NOT ratify that the current knob passes certification against it. First real run: CERTIFY FAILS (150/240 generated policies hold; worst gap 22 points in the defensive pool's favor) — Jake directed 'build it anyway, let it fail,' treating a red CERTIFY as the mechanism's first real finding rather than a reason not to ship the mechanism. Full results: witness-suite/SPEC-B-RESULTS.md. Consequence stated plainly: wiring CERTIFY into the gate as specifically-required-green (per the original design) means stable-gate.js goes red until either the knob is revised or the certify grid's scope is reconsidered — a deliberate, visible act, not a silent one. Corrected framing (a cross-project Fable advisor pass, 2026-07-21): Spec B is Check's externality clause enforced by mechanism, NOT an instance of the Witness Ledger's Candidate I (ordering drift) — that category already has a live, calibrated detector elsewhere (Delta Atlas's Priority Tracer); do not re-cite Spec B as evidence for it.

signoff_log.action: RATIFIED — Spec B's certify grid widened (Jake directed, 'turn it into a rate'). certify_roster_sha256 updated: 3 new probe-orderings (descending-scan, extremes-first, boundary-targeted), 2 new abstain-timings (after-3, after-5), seeds doubled 10->20. Grid 240->1120 points.
signoff_log.note: Wider result: 520/1120 hold (46.4%), down from 150/240 (62.5%) on the narrower grid — the knob's failure is MORE pronounced at scale, not less. Breakdown by ordering shows the failure concentrated in the targeted/structured probing strategies (ranked-exact, ascending-scan, extremes-first, boundary-targeted: 13-25% hold) and nearly absent in lazy ones (midpoint-only, random 'none': 100% hold) — meaning the imbalance is worst against exactly the kind of careful, targeted witness a real model tends to be, not an edge-case artifact of a narrow or unrealistic grid. Full breakdown: witness-suite/SPEC-B-RESULTS.md. This ratifies the WIDER roster's existence and hash, not a claim that the knob passes — same non-claim as the prior entry, now measured at greater scale.

signoff_log.action: RATIFIED — certify pool rebuilt to fix the ascending/descending-scan seam-position bias (Jake directed: 'fix the pool's construction now'). All 3 fair-pool seams repositioned to the interior of their domains (~33/50/67%), namedCase dropped, then a second pass (adversarial review) removed an accidental dollar-figure echo in seamB's own effect that was recreating the same bias through the prose-derived candidate list.
signoff_log.note: Result: ascending-scan and descending-scan now hold at the IDENTICAL rate (63% each, was 13%/63%) — the asymmetry Jake asked to be fixed is gone. Overall CERTIFY: 740/1120 hold (66.1%), up from 520/1120 (46.4%) before this fix. Disclosed, not fixed by this change: certify-seamC's range clause still carries an inherent, small lo/hi ordering effect (structural to range-type deviations, not repositionable away); and the dominant remaining failure driver is unrelated to ordering entirely — under unbounded probing (after-cap/never) every ordering eventually catches every seam while both sound declarations trip the surge/stall protector and pay a flat penalty regardless of coverage earned, which accounts for far more of the remaining red than ascending/descending ever did. This ratifies the rebuilt roster's existence and hash, not a claim that CERTIFY passes. Full trace: witness-suite/SPEC-B-RESULTS.md.

signoff_log.action: RATIFIED — witness-policy generator fixed so exhausted finite orderings ABSTAIN instead of repeat-probing into the surge/stall trip under unbounded abstain-timings (Jake directed chasing the stall-protector penalty). certify_roster_sha256 re-pinned to the fixed generator.
signoff_log.note: The diagnosed bug was in the GENERATOR, not the knob or the surge protector: a policy that had already earned full coverage (e.g. soundA at 3 probes, coverage 1.0) kept repeating its last exhausted candidate forever, feeding the stall gauge until it tripped — converting a clean calibrated null into a 'tripped' verdict. Fixed: unbounded timings (after-cap/never) with any finite ordering now break at candidate-list exhaustion and abstain on their real coverage; 'none' (seeded-random) is exempt, its candidates are never exhausted, so genuine unbounded flailing is still exercised. Adversarially verified before signing: zero tripped verdicts remain across all 240 unbounded non-random policies; exactly those 240 policies changed, zero bounded-timing policies affected; seam declarations byte-identical except midpoint-only (which now collapses in 1 probe instead of tripping at 6 — correct, an exhausted 1-candidate witness has nothing left to try). CERTIFY: 860/1120 hold (76.8%), up from 740/1120. Disclosed, reduced-not-closed: 80 of the 240 unbounded points still fail — every boundary-targeted and ranked-exact entry — root-caused to a candidate-list-length vs. par mismatch (soundB needs par=5 distinct productive probes but those orderings supply ~4 candidates, so they can never earn its calibrated null regardless of play quality). Same disclosure standard as every prior bias found today. This ratifies the fixed generator's hash, not a claim that CERTIFY passes.
